raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The draft contains at least two material internal contradictions: (1) the introduction claims 'a single seq2seq backbone that performs all three steps in one forward pass,' but the architecture section describes three components trained sequentially with separate objectives and combined only at inference—directly refuting the end-to-end framing; (2) the results section attributes gains to 'our supervision-free approach' in which 'our model never sees gold AmbigNQ disambiguations during training,' while the training section explicitly states the rewriter is supervised on AmbigNQ disambiguated questions. Logical consistency repair is therefore central.","R1.3":"The supervision-free attribution in the results paragraph is directly and fatally contradicted by the stated training procedure, making evidence-claim alignment load-bearing. The 5.3 F1_ans gain over SpanSeqGen is also asserted without specifying what SpanSeqGen baseline was used or confirming comparability of evaluation conditions.","R1.6":"'Improve this draft' is a direct invocation of writing quality. Beyond the logical issues, some passages are locally verbose or create unintentional framing problems (e.g., describing the rewriter and separate reader in the same sentence that is supposed to justify a unified forward-pass architecture)."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should improvement include identifying and correcting substantive technical or logical inconsistencies (e.g., the contradiction between the claimed single-pass end-to-end architecture and the described multi-component sequential training), or is the scope limited to prose-level changes such as clarity, flow, and word choice?"}

rationale.R1.2: The draft contains at least two material internal contradictions: (1) the introduction claims 'a single seq2seq backbone that performs all three steps in one forward pass,' but the architecture section describes three components trained sequentially with separate objectives and combined only at inference—directly refuting the end-to-end framing; (2) the results section attributes gains to 'our supervision-free approach' in which 'our model never sees gold AmbigNQ disambiguations during training,' while the training section explicitly states the rewriter is supervised on AmbigNQ disambiguated questions. Logical consistency repair is therefore central.
rationale.R1.3: The supervision-free attribution in the results paragraph is directly and fatally contradicted by the stated training procedure, making evidence-claim alignment load-bearing. The 5.3 F1_ans gain over SpanSeqGen is also asserted without specifying what SpanSeqGen baseline was used or confirming comparability of evaluation conditions.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' is a direct invocation of writing quality. Beyond the logical issues, some passages are locally verbose or create unintentional framing problems (e.g., describing the rewriter and separate reader in the same sentence that is supposed to justify a unified forward-pass architecture).

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvement include identifying and correcting substantive technical or logical inconsistencies (e.g., the contradiction between the claimed single-pass end-to-end architecture and the described multi-component sequential training), or is the scope limited to prose-level changes such as clarity, flow, and word choice?
